question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to accept a fraudulent stream file by injecting a crafted StreamFileSignature whose node reports an artificially low totalStake as the first element in the collection, so that getStakeRequiredForConsensus() computes a threshold far below the real network stake, allowing a minority of colluding nodes to pass consensus and corrupt the ledger view, leading to direct loss of funds for users relying on balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that totalStake is read exclusively from the first signature's node (line 45) rather than being validated against all nodes in the collection, so that by controlling the ordering of the signatures collection an attacker lowers the consensus threshold and causes a fraudulent stream file to be accepted, resulting in incorrect transaction records and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the consensusRatio == ZERO bypass at line 54 by observing or influencing the commonDownloaderProperties configuration such that any non-empty set of VERIFIED signatures causes validate() to return early without checking stake thresholds, allowing a single compromised node's signature to be treated as consensus and a malicious stream file to be ingested, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio()) check at line 54, which is scale-sensitive (BigDecimal '0.0' does not equal BigDecimal '0' via .equals()), so that a consensusRatio stored as 0.0 or 0.00 bypasses the early-return guard and the full consensus check is skipped, allowing fraudulent stream files to be accepted and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long integer overflow in the stake accumulation loop at line 67 (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by supplying signatures whose individual node stakes sum beyond Long.MAX_VALUE, wrapping the accumulated stake to a negative or artificially small value, causing canReachConsensus() to return false for a legitimate majority and allowing a minority hash group to be the only one that passes, corrupting the accepted stream file and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply StreamFileSignature objects with a null fileHash field, causing getFileHashAsHex() to return null, so that all null-fileHash signatures are grouped under the same null key in the HashMultimap at line 50, artificially inflating the apparent stake for a single hash group and causing canReachConsensus() to return true for a fraudulent file hash, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that consensusCount is incremented by validatedSignatures.size() (line 71) rather than by stake weight, so that a single hash group with even one VERIFIED signature reaching the stake threshold sets consensusCount > 0 and causes all other hash groups' signatures to remain unvalidated, allowing a minority-stake fraudulent file to be accepted if it is the only group meeting the threshold, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to throw a SignatureVerificationException with the message 'Invalid total staking weight' by ensuring the first signature's node reports totalStake == 0, which halts the entire validation pipeline and causes a denial-of-service on stream file ingestion, preventing legitimate transactions from being recorded and indirectly causing loss of funds for time-sensitive operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative totalStake value from the first node (since long can be negative), causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a negative stakeRequiredForConsensus via BigDecimal multiplication, so that canReachConsensus() at line 89 always returns true (any stake >= negative threshold), allowing any single VERIFIED signature to pass consensus regardless of actual network agreement, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the >= comparison in canReachConsensus() at line 89 combined with RoundingMode.CEILING in getStakeRequiredForConsensus() at line 99, such that the ceiling rounding raises the threshold by 1 unit and a legitimate majority stake that is exactly at the 1/3 boundary fails consensus while a fraudulent minority group with slightly higher stake passes, causing the wrong stream file to be accepted and leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signatures collection where all signatures share the same fileHash (including a fraudulent one) and all have VERIFIED status, so that the single hash group accumulates all node stakes, passes canReachConsensus(), and the fraudulent file hash is accepted as consensus without any competing legitimate hash group being present, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stakeRequiredForConsensus is initialized to null at line 39 and only set when the first signature is processed, so that if the signatures collection is empty the variable remains null, and any code path that reaches canReachConsensus() with a null stakeRequiredForConsensus throws a NullPointerException that is not a SignatureVerificationException, potentially bypassing upstream error handling and causing undefined behavior in stream file acceptance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the validate() method to accept a fraudulent stream file by supplying a collection where the first signature belongs to a node with a very high totalStake (making the threshold high) but subsequent signatures all belong to nodes with zero individual stake, so that the legitimate hash group never reaches canReachConsensus() while a fraudulent group with one high-stake node does, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inconsistency that totalStake is read only from the first signature's node (line 45) while individual stakes are read from each signature's own node (line 67), so that nodes can report inconsistent stake values — a low totalStake from the first node and high individual stakes from subsequent nodes — causing canReachConsensus() to trivially pass with a fraudulent file hash, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject duplicate StreamFileSignature objects (same node, same filename, different fileHash) into the collection, exploiting HashMultimap's set-based deduplication behavior, to split legitimate stake across multiple hash groups so that no single group reaches the consensus threshold, causing a denial-of-service on stream file ingestion and preventing legitimate transactions from being recorded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method sets CONSENSUS_REACHED status on signatures via a forEach lambda at line 72 (validatedSignatures.forEach(s -> s.setStatus(CONSENSUS_REACHED))), and since StreamFileSignature is mutable, a race condition in a concurrent environment could allow a fraudulent signature to have its status set to CONSENSUS_REACHED before the downstream verifySignatures() check, causing a malicious stream file to be processed and leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where only one hash group exists and it contains exactly one VERIFIED signature from a node whose stake equals or exceeds the computed stakeRequiredForConsensus, so that a single compromised node can unilaterally pass consensus validation and cause a fraudulent stream file to be accepted, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the consensusRatio bypass at line 54 only checks signatureHashMap.isEmpty() (i.e., whether any VERIFIED signatures exist) rather than checking whether the verified signatures represent a sufficient number of distinct nodes, so that a single VERIFIED signature with any fileHash triggers the bypass when consensusRatio is zero, allowing a single-node fraudulent file to be accepted and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the validate() method to accept a fraudulent stream file by providing a collection where all legitimate signatures have status DOWNLOADED (not VERIFIED), so that signatureHashMap remains empty, and then exploiting a separate code path that does not re-invoke validate() after signatures are re-verified, causing the fraudulent file to be ingested without consensus, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the debugStake variable at line 59 tracks the maximum stake seen across all hash groups and is included in the SignatureVerificationException message at line 85, leaking internal stake distribution information that an attacker can use to calibrate future attacks on the consensus threshold, ultimately enabling a targeted attack that causes direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable state of StreamFileSignature objects passed into validate(), where the method mutates signature statuses via setStatus() at line 72, so that if validate() is called concurrently on overlapping signature collections, a signature's status could be set to CONSENSUS_REACHED by one thread while another thread is still evaluating it, causing a fraudulent file to be accepted in a race condition and leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to return BigDecimal.ZERO by supplying a totalStake value of 1 combined with a consensusRatio that rounds down to zero after CEILING rounding, so that canReachConsensus() returns true for any non-negative stake value including zero, allowing a fraudulent stream file with zero-stake nodes to pass consensus and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method iterates over signatureHashMap.keySet() at line 62 and for each key calls signatureHashMap.get(key) at line 63, and since HashMultimap is not thread-safe, a concurrent modification during iteration could cause a ConcurrentModificationException or skip entries, leading to incorrect consensus evaluation and potential acceptance of a fraudulent stream file causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a fileHash that is an empty byte array (length 0), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string '', so that all empty-fileHash signatures are grouped under the '' key in HashMultimap, artificially concentrating stake under a single group that may not represent a legitimate file hash, allowing a fraudulent consensus to be reached and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method only checks consensusCount > 0 at line 80 (not that consensusCount meets a minimum node count), so that a single hash group with one VERIFIED high-stake node sets consensusCount to 1 and passes validation, even if all other nodes disagree, allowing a single-node fraudulent consensus and causing direct loss of funds?
